findings.problem: The chapter's causal objective lands, but scene economy is lopsided: procedural loops dominate the middle while post-proof interpersonal consequence is comparatively compressed, reducing end-of-chapter dramatic leverage.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten repetitive transaction beats in the records-office run (especially snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:114, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:154, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:214) and reallocate space to one additional consequential choice beat in the aftermath section (snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:291, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:371) that changes what Marisol can or cannot do next chapter.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised chapter still includes all mandatory retrieval beats (wrong form, stigma field confrontation, AR-17 workaround, certified reveal) and also adds one explicit same-day consequence action after proof acquisition (direct contact attempt, boundary breach, or irreversible decision) before the handoff line at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:392; FAIL if aftermath remains planning-only.

findings.problem: Several policy-heavy stretches drift into process explanation mode, weakening embodied consequence and rhythm at moments that should carry maximum pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:154, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:170, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:198, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:257, keep only the minimum procedural language needed for causality and pair each retained rule statement with immediate sensory or psychological fallout in Marisol's close-third perspective.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if each cited block contains no more than one consecutive sentence of pure procedural exposition before a concrete body/thought/action beat from Marisol; FAIL if two or more policy-only sentences run back-to-back without interior consequence in those spans.

findings.problem: Timidity: after the witness-line reveal, the narrative repeatedly shifts into containment logistics (routing, packet assembly, delay) instead of forcing a fresh interpersonal collision, which softens the chapter's peak consequence.
findings.rewrite_direction: In the post-train section (snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:327, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:335, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:365, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:392), add one failed or costly direct outreach beat (to Gloria, Carina, or Ada) that keeps sobriety stakes active while preserving the chapter-12 handoff.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passage before snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:392 contains one concrete outreach attempt with immediate pushback or destabilizing consequence; FAIL if the sequence remains limited to planning, filing, and meeting attendance without new relational blowback.

findings.problem: Imelda and Ms. Chao both default to similar clipped procedural diction across key exchanges; idiolect separation weakens and risks institutional voice blur.
findings.rewrite_direction: Differentiate clerk registers at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:170, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:174, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:196, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:210, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:257: keep Imelda's weary, human workaround cadence and make Ms. Chao more technical, sparse, and audit-driven, with distinct rhythm and vocabulary choices that cannot be swapped.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if each clerk has at least two dialogue lines in the cited spans with distinct lexical/rhythmic signatures and at least one leverage-shift question/response; FAIL if lines can be reassigned between Imelda and Ms. Chao with little or no perceived character change.

findings.problem: The chapter reuses the same comparative simile scaffold ("X like Y") more than twice at high-pressure beats, creating detectable formula repetition.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise at least three of the cited lines (especially snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:64, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:216, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:355) to deliver force through concrete verbs, texture, and spatial pressure rather than comparative template language.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if no more than two of the cited moments rely on explicit "like" simile construction after revision; FAIL if three or more still use the same comparative template in those locations.

findings.problem: Emotion delivery overuses the "Marisol felt..." construction, which reads as mechanical signaling rather than scene-specific embodiment.
findings.rewrite_direction: At the cited lines, replace at least two "Marisol felt..." turns with action-led or dialogue-adjacent physiological cues that externalize stress without announcing emotion.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if at least two of the four cited beats no longer use "Marisol felt..." and still clearly communicate her emotional state through observable behavior or bodily response; FAIL if three or more cited beats retain the same lead-in construction.

summary: Chapter 11 is causally coherent and continuity-aligned (date, cause, witness, tense, and close-third focalization all track canon), but it does not clear pass-level craft threshold due scene-economy imbalance, procedural-over-embodiment drift, dialogue voice blur in institutional exchanges, and repeated prose formulas that flatten line-level freshness.
